compiled_state.title: [end-session Step 5]: .checkpoint-needs-review and .checkpoint-pending-rollback surfaced as untracked files requiring action
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

During Step 5 (Verify Clean Worktree State) or Step 4.75 in end-session, the files `.checkpoint-needs-review` and `.checkpoint-pending-rollback` appear in `git status --porcelain` as untracked files, causing the skill to surface them as failures requiring user action.

## Actual Behavior

Agent reports these files as untracked and asks for action:
  'The .checkpoint-needs-review and .checkpoint-pending-rollback files are untracked checkpoint artifacts — no action needed on them.'

## Expected Behavior

Checkpoint artifact files (`.checkpoint-needs-review`, `.checkpoint-pending-rollback`) should be silently ignored during the Step 4.75 / Step 5 clean-worktree check — either by adding them to `.gitignore`, or by filtering them from the porcelain output before evaluating the is_clean condition.

## Suggested Fix

Add `.checkpoint-needs-review` and `.checkpoint-pending-rollback` to the repo `.gitignore`, or filter them in the end-session skill's clean-worktree check.

comments.body: Mechanical fix: added .checkpoint-needs-review and .checkpoint-pending-rollback to .gitignore. These files are read directly by skip-review-check.sh from filesystem — gitignore does not affect review gate enforcement.
comments.author: Test
